entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop essential for food and nutritional security worldwide.

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITIONAL SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food.

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by living organisms such as pests and pathogens that affect crop productivity.

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by non-living factors such as drought or salinity that affect crop productivity.

entities.name: STRESS RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop variety to maintain productivity under stress environments.

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING
entities.description: Traditional crop improvement approaches based on phenotypic selection.

entities.name: GENOMIC-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: An approach combining conventional breeding with genome information to accelerate crop improvement.

entities.name: SEQUENCING TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Technology used to determine the nucleotide sequence of DNA, reducing costs and increasing genomic data.

entities.name: GENOMIC RESOURCES
entities.description: The collection of genetic data, maps, and markers available for a specific crop.

entities.name: MARKERS
entities.description: DNA sequences used to identify specific locations on a chromosome for mapping and selection.

entities.name: LINKAGE MAPPING
entities.description: A method used to determine the relative positions of genes or markers on a chromosome.

entities.name: ASSOCIATION MAPPING
entities.description: A method used to link genetic markers to specific traits based on population-wide associations.

entities.name: MARKER ASSISTED BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A genomic-assisted breeding technique used to transfer a specific trait into a recipient variety using markers.

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A genomic-assisted breeding technique that uses genome-wide markers to predict the breeding value of individuals.

entities.name: GENETIC GAINS
entities.description: The increase in the average performance of a crop population over time through breeding efforts.
